resources.string: Please input an apk version for patching. Partial version names are allowed, such as:\n\"420\", \"420.15.2\", or full \"420.15.2.11\"
Pick a server for APK downloads.
Enable if you can\'t remove the preinstalled Meta apps.
Enable if you can\'t remove the original Messenger app.
Use an alternative fallback patching method.
Set a custom keystore for apk signing.
Allow third-party modules to be loaded.
Remove temporary files from the cache.
"This will not delete your patched apps."

Enabling this option lets you install patched Messenger alongside original Meta apps. \nHowever, if you have the option to uninstall both Facebook and Messenger, but are facing issues due to other pre-installed apps from Meta, it is highly recommended to uninstall both original apps and install the patched versions instead. This fixes cross-app features like opening links from Messenger to Facebook and login sharing.

This option is NOT recommended for regular users. It is meant for those who cannot remove the original Messenger app. \nNote that enabling this option may affect some functionality in Messenger and cause minor issues. Please try to remove the original Messenger app before using this option. \n[See \"Resolve apk conflicts\" option]
Enable fallback mode? \n[Apps patched in fallback mode may launch slightly slower]

Instructions: 

Uninstall all official facebook apps: facebook, messenger, facebook lite, etc. 

Install the ChatHeadEnabler module. 

Patch and install a Messenger apk from here. 

Please watch the video tutorial (from the app menu) for complete instructions. 

[Find the help forum under Settings.]

Troubleshoot: 

Download failed? 
Select a different server or try again later. 
Alternatively, you can download a Messenger apk from apkmirror.com and patch it using the manual option. 

Install failed? 
Make sure you have uninstalled all official Meta apps. If some Meta apps cannot be uninstalled, enable the "Resolve apk conflicts" option and try patching again. 

Not working? 
Make sure you have installed the ChatHeadEnabler module. Also, try restarting the Messenger app. 

[Find the help forum under Settings.]

Safety Note: 

Is it safe to use? 
Yes. It is just as safe as any app installed from the Play Store. 
(Make sure you downloaded MRVPatchManager from the official GitHub link.) 

How can I be sure? 
All the apps, tools and dependencies used here are open source. You can view their full source code on GitHub. 

What safety measures are taken? 
MRVPatchManager downloads your selected app from various APK servers and verifies it against the original Meta signature, ensuring you are patching a genuine, official Meta app. 
Because of this local signature verification, APK servers cannot distribute tampered or unsafe files. If a file has been modified, the signature check fails and the manager will refuse to patch it. This mirrors the exact security check the Play Store uses to verify an app's authenticity."
